prompt: Ultra-realistic 16:9 close-up flat studio photograph of a Japanese residence card (在留カード) placed on a clean white or soft neutral background. The card is horizontally aligned, centered in frame, captured in sharp macro focus with soft diffused lighting and subtle realistic shadows. The design follows an authentic Japanese immigration and Ministry of Justice identity document aesthetic with minimalistic layout, precise typography, muted pastel colors, and realistic anti-counterfeit detailing. Top header contains bold Japanese text “在留カード” with smaller English subtitle “RESIDENCE CARD.” Top-left corner displays “日本国政府 / GOVERNMENT OF JAPAN.” Top-right contains a realistic alphanumeric card number. The card contains a passport-style portrait of a young Japanese woman: – Fair East Asian skin tone with natural texture – Straight black hair with soft bangs and shoulder-length styling – Dark brown eyes, neutral expression, direct gaze – Minimal makeup, realistic facial details – Wearing a dark navy blazer over a white shirt Identity information displayed in bilingual Japanese-English format: – Name: YAMADA HANAKO – Date of Birth: 1990/05/01 – Sex: F – Nationality: Japan – Address: Tokyo Minato-ku – Status: Engineer – Permission to work: Allowed – Period of stay: 5 years – Date of Issue: 2023/05/01 – Date of Expiration: 2028/05/01 Security and design features include subtle guilloche patterns, microprinting, pastel gradients, and official hanko stamps. Material appearance is matte laminated polycarbonate with realistic ink texture. Style: photorealistic, authentic Japanese government document aesthetic, macro clarity, 8K resolution.
